code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}" if "," not in o else f"serves {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"with ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"with location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"with IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda o: f"known locally as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda o: f"the leader is from the {o} party",
        "capital": lambda o: f"has the capital of {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o.replace(' languages','').replace(' language','')}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"is led by chief {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has its headquarters at {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has largest city {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway number {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has {o} as its largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a first runway number {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"within the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"with mayor {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has runway surface {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"the official language is {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"based in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"under the jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"the people are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
        "class": lambda o: f"classified as {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"division is {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"order is {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"with hub at {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"participated in {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"has the county seat of {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"with postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"with area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject and predicate, combining multiple objects for the same predicate
    from collections import defaultdict
    # subj -> predicate -> list of objects
    subj_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        subj_pred_objs[t.subject][t.predicate].append(t.object)

    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        phrases: list[str] = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            # Remove duplicate objects while preserving order
            seen = set()
            unique_objs = [x for x in objs if not (x in seen or seen.add(x))]
            generator = phrase_map.get(pred)
            if generator:
                if len(unique_objs) == 1:
                    phrase = generator(unique_objs[0])
                else:
                    combined_obj = " and ".join(unique_objs)
                    phrase = generator(combined_obj)
            else:
                phrase = f"{pred} {' and '.join(unique_objs)}"
            phrases.append(phrase)
        subject_phrases[subj] = phrases

    # Build clauses (combine clauses with commas and an Oxford comma)
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}")
        else:
            clauses.append(f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1])

    # Try to connect subjects into a single flowing sentence where relationships exist
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        result = clauses[0]
    else:
        # Connect clauses with relative pronouns where possible for a single sentence
        result = clauses[0]
        for i in range(1, len(clauses)):
            # Check if current clause subject appears in previous text (for relative clause connection)
            current_clause = clauses[i]
            # Extract subject from current clause (first word before space)
            current_subj = current_clause.split()[0]

            # Check if this subject was mentioned before in a way we can use relative clause
            prev_text = result
            if f" in {current_subj}" in prev_text or f" of {current_subj}" in prev_text:
                # Use relative clause: "which is" or "where"
                if " in " in prev_text and prev_text.endswith(f" in {current_subj}"):
                    result = result[:-len(f" in {current_subj}")] + f", where {current_clause[len(current_subj)+1:]}"
                else:
                    result = result + f", which {current_clause[len(current_subj)+1:]}"
            else:
                # Use semicolon as fallback
                result = result + "; " + current_clause

    # Capitalize first letter and add period
    if result:
        result = result[0].upper() + result[1:]
        if not result.endswith("."):
            result = result + "."
    return result if result else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
